title: Imperator-class Star Destroyer

{{Quote|Noticeably larger and more heavily armed than the Venator, this new ship saw only very limited service before the war ended. But as far as the newly declared Empire was concerned, it represented the future.|Historian Beaumont Kin in ''The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire''|<ref name="Rise">''[[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Imperator''-class Star Destroyer''' was a class of [[Star Destroyer]] that was manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards]] in [[19 BBY]] and pressed into service late into the [[Clone Wars]] with the [[Galactic Republic]]. Appearing in small numbers, it witnessed limited service before the [[Mission to Mustafar|end]] of the war. After the war, Kuat Drive Yards reworked the design of the ''Imperator''-class into the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], which became the centerpiece of the [[Imperial Navy]]. The [[historian]] [[Beaumont Kin]] wrote about the ''Imperator''-class in his book ''[[The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]''.

==Characteristics==
The ''Imperator''-class [[Star Destroyer]] was manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards]] to serve as a massive, powerful, and multi-functional [[warship]].<ref name="BMF 23" /> It was one of the largest warships in the [[Republic Navy]].<ref name="BMF 20" /> It was larger and more heavily armed than the [[Venator-class Star Destroyer|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]].<ref name="Rise" />

==History==
[[File:StarDestroyer-Fathead.png|thumb|right|280px|The ''Imperator''-class was modified and refined into its successor, the ''Imperial''-class (pictured).]]
{{Quote|The new ''Imperator''-class Star Destroyer began to exit KDY facilities toward the very end of the Clone Wars.|Historian Beaumont Kin in ''The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire''|<ref name="Rise" />}}
In [[19 BBY]], KDY built upon the design of the [[Venator-class Star Destroyer|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] to create the ''Imperator''-class,<ref name="Timelines" /> which was additionally designed by the [[Galactic Republic]]. With the design completed in the same [[Standard year|year]], the ''Imperator''-class Star Destroyer was introduced near the end of the [[Clone Wars]],<ref name="BMF 20" /> appearing in small numbers<ref name="BMF 23" /> and witnessing limited service before the [[Mission to Mustafar|end]] of the conflict.<ref name="BMF 20" /> Following the [[Battle of Coruscant]], and the [[Proclamation of the New Order|transformation]] of the Republic into the [[Galactic Empire]], KDY was contracted as the exclusive Imperial supplier of Star Destroyers for the [[Imperial Navy]].<ref name="Timelines" />

Later in 19 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]], KDY reworked the ''Imperator''-class<ref name="Timelines" /> and re-christened it into the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial''-class]],<ref name="Rise" /> and production of the refined Star Destroyer was increased under the Galactic Empire. The ''Imperial''-class became a mainstay of the Imperial Navy, and it went on to have a distinguished career. As it was capable of laying waste to entire [[planet]]s, the ''Imperial''-class became the prime enforcer of the Empire and rendered its predecessor, the ''Imperator''-class, obsolete.<ref name="BMF 23" />

In [[35 ABY]],<ref name="RiseDate">{{RiseAndFallDate}}</ref> the [[historian]] and [[Resistance]] paramilitary member [[Beaumont Kin]] mentioned the ''Imperator''-class Star Destroyer in several instances in<ref name="Rise" /> his<ref name="TROSVD">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> [[book]] ''[[The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]''. Surviving records from KDY were secretive of when they were informed of the forthcoming change of ''Imperator'' to ''Imperial'', to which Kin questioned if KDY was already aware of what was soon to transpire on the [[Galaxy|galactic]] geopolitical level. Kin later cited a redacted file on titled "Renaming Protocol" in the ''Imperator''-class Star Destroyer section of the [[Kuat Archives]].<ref name="Rise" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:BTMF20.png|thumb|right|200px|In the current canon, ''Imperator''-class Star Destroyers were introduced in ''Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon'' 20.]]
The ''Imperator''-class Star Destroyer was first mentioned in the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the "[[Starship Fact File]]" section of the [[Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 20|twentieth issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon]]'' magazine,<ref name="BMF 20" /> which was released around [[May 20]], [[2015]]<ref name="BMF 20 Date">{{BMFdates|20}}</ref> and written by [[Mark Newbold]].<ref name="SM">{{DMCite|dm=File:MarkNewbold-CreditsBMF.png|creator=Mark Newbold}}</ref> In the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, the ''Imperator''-class [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] was the initial name of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]], as established by the [[2005]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith Incredible Cross-Sections]]'', which was authored by [[Curtis Saxton]].<ref name="Revenge of the Sith ICS">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref> In [[Wikipedia:Latin|Latin]], the word "imperātor" means "commander," "general," or "emperor."<ref name="Collins Latin Dictionary">{{BookCite|container=Collins Dictionaries|title=Collins Latin Dictionary and Grammar|version=2nd ed.|publisher=[[Wikipedia:HarperCollins|HarperCollins Publishers]]|date=[[2016]]|isbn=978-0008167677}}</ref>

Although established to be a separate class of Star Destroyer, the [[Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 74|seventy-fourth]] issue of the magazine states that the ''Imperial''-class was originally known as the ''Imperator''-class,<ref name="BMF 74">{{BuildFalconCite|74|Secrets of Spaceflight}}</ref> as does "[[Starships of the Empire and the Rebellion]]."<ref name="SER">{{EncyclopediaCite|Starships of the Empire and the Rebellion}}</ref> Since ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'', released in [[2023]], reconfirms that the ''Imperator''-class was a separate Star Destroyer,<ref name="Timelines" /> this article assumes ''Build the Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} seventy-fourth issue and [[Star Wars Encyclopedia (series)|''Star Wars Encyclopedia'']] is incorrect.
